top_stories.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
top_stories.summary: Microsoft will begin rolling out passkey support for Microsoft Entra-protected resources on Windows devices starting late April. The feature enables phishing-resistant passwordless authentication for Entra-managed environments.

top_stories.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
top_stories.summary: Okta-targeted vishing attacks are exploiting IT helpdesk staff through impersonation and urgency tactics to bypass identity controls. These social engineering campaigns manipulate support personnel into granting access, enabling attackers to rapidly escalate privileges and trigger large-scale data breaches.

top_stories.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
top_stories.summary: A 2025 security analysis identifies growing attacker focus on exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows alongside phishing campaigns that leverage compromised but valid credentials from trusted accounts. The report highlights how both attack vectors specifically target authentication trust mechanisms rather than bypassing security controls entirely.

top_stories.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
top_stories.summary: DORA's Article 9 establishes authentication and access control as legal obligations for EU financial entities. The article examines the specific credential management and access control requirements under the regulation and the consequences of non-compliance.

top_stories.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
top_stories.summary: The Bitwarden CLI npm package was compromised by attackers who uploaded a malicious version to the npm registry containing a credential-stealing payload. The package targeted developer credentials and had the capability to spread to other projects through the npm dependency chain.

news.title: Users advised to drop passwords and make room for passkeys
news.summary: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) is officially recommending that consumers replace passwords with passkeys, reversing decades of prior guidance. The agency stated it will no longer recommend passwords where passkeys are available, citing passwords' weaker resistance to modern cyber threats.

news.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
news.summary: The article addresses the governance gap created by AI agents operating as delegated actors within enterprise security frameworks, focusing on how authority and access management apply to agentic AI systems. The piece covers concepts of delegation, provisioning, and continuous observability as mechanisms for governing AI agent permissions and actions.

news.title: The New World ID: Proof of Human for the AI Era Scales Across the Digital Platforms People and Businesses Use Every Day
news.summary: World announced the next generation of World ID, a proof-of-human protocol, with Okta among the partners bringing the technology to businesses, AI agents, and consumers. The integration with Okta and other major platforms positions World ID as an identity verification layer aimed at distinguishing humans from AI agents across digital services.

news.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
news.summary: A threat group called UNC6692 is using Microsoft Teams to impersonate IT help desk personnel and deploy the SNOW malware suite through social engineering. The attack vector relies on convincing victims to accept Teams chat invitations from fake IT support accounts, a technique increasingly relevant to IAM and identity verification practices.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI package (@bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0) was compromised as part of an ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, with malicious code injected into a file called 'bw1.js.' The compromise was discovered by JFrog and Socket, targeting the command-line interface of the widely used Bitwarden password manager.

news.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
news.summary: Vercel disclosed that additional customer accounts were compromised in a breach involving unauthorized access to its internal systems, linked to a Context.ai security incident. The expanded investigation reviewed network requests and additional compromise indicators to identify the affected accounts.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing that regular password resets can be exploited by attackers through helpdesk social engineering, enabling full account compromise. The analysis focuses on how reset procedures can bypass security controls when helpdesk staff are manipulated into processing fraudulent reset requests.

news.title: Consumer Identity and Access Management Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2036, Profiles of Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, Amazon Web Services, Accenture
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global Consumer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) market will grow from USD 12.6 billion in 2026 to USD 40.2 billion by 2036, representing a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers key vendors including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, AWS, and Accenture.

news.title: Non-Human Identity Access Management Market Global Forecast Report 2026-2036: Opportunities in Adoption of Zero Trust Architecture, Cloud-native Applications, and Regulatory Compliance Requirements
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global non-human identity (NHI) access management market will grow from USD 12.2 billion in 2026 to USD 38.8 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers market dynamics across regions and sectors, with growth drivers identified as zero trust architecture adoption, cloud-native applications, and regulatory compliance requirements.

news.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
news.summary: The article examines identity-based attacks as a primary initial access vector, focusing on how attackers use stolen credentials rather than technical exploits to breach organizations. It covers credential stuffing and related techniques that bypass traditional security controls by abusing legitimate authentication pathways.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in SMS phishing attacks in 2022. The attacks compromised at least a dozen major technology companies through credential phishing, enabling cryptocurrency theft from investors.

news.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
news.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool led to a data breach via stolen OAuth tokens. Security researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging primary attack surface for lateral movement in enterprise environments.

news.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
news.summary: Microsoft published details on how it secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform by removing credentials and reducing attack surfaces through platform engineering. The approach focuses on eliminating standing credentials and minimizing exposure to opportunistic threats as part of Microsoft's secure-by-design initiative.

news.title: Securely connecting on-premises data systems to Amazon Redshift with IAM Roles Anywhere
news.summary: AWS published a guide explaining how to use IAM Roles Anywhere with Amazon Redshift to establish secure, private connections for on-premises data systems. The solution eliminates the need for long-lived access keys by leveraging certificate-based authentication to obtain temporary IAM credentials.

news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
news.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing attacks. The technique exploits legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to trick victims into granting attackers access to their accounts, bypassing MFA protections.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical security scenarios. It focuses on how threat actors bypass security systems outside conventional IT settings and how 2FA could provide an additional barrier in physical-world contexts.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user sessions.
